mnemonics.title: EEE - Explore, Exploit, Evaluate
mnemonics.content: Remember the RL cycle: First EXPLORE by trying different arms, then EXPLOIT by choosing the best-known arm, then EVALUATE by updating your knowledge based on rewards received.

mnemonics.title: UCB Formula Parts
mnemonics.content: UCB = Q + C × √(log time / count). Think: 'Quality plus Curiosity increases with log time but decreases with count (how much we know)'.

mnemonics.title: Thompson Sampling Process
mnemonics.content: Remember the 4 steps: 1) POSTERIOR (maintain distributions), 2) SAMPLE (draw from each), 3) SELECT (pick max), 4) UPDATE (Bayes' rule). Use first letters: P-S-S-U.

mnemonics.title: Epsilon-Greedy
mnemonics.content: Epsilon (ε) represents 'exploration rate'. When ε=0.1, think: '10% chance to explore, 90% to exploit - keep it small so mostly exploit!'

mnemonics.title: Bandit Algorithm Progression
mnemonics.content: From simplest to most sophisticated: EG (Epsilon-Greedy) → UCB (Upper Confidence Bound) → TS (Thompson Sampling). Think: 'Easy to Understand, Complex Solutions'.
